input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML**
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.Views.ItemsPage">

    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"
                    IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"
                    RemainingItemsThreshold="0">

        <CollectionView.Header>
            <RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
                         IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">
                <!-- wrapping trick not needed; see below -->
            </RefreshView>
        </CollectionView.Header>

        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate>
                <VerticalStackLayout Padding="12">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="18" />
                    <Label Text="{Binding Description}" FontSize="14"
                           TextColor="Gray" />
                </VerticalStackLayout>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>
</ContentPage>
```

The idiomatic approach is to wrap the **entire CollectionView** inside a `RefreshView`:

```xml
<RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
             IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">

    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}">
        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate>
                <VerticalStackLayout Padding="12">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="18" />
                    <Label Text="{Binding Description}"
                           FontSize="14" TextColor="Gray" />
                </VerticalStackLayout>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>

</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel** (using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm)
```csharp
public partial class ItemsViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IApiService _api;
    private readonly INavigationService _nav;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ObservableCollection<Item> _items = new();

    [ObservableProperty]
    private bool _isRefreshing;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private Item? _selectedItem;

    // Fires automatically when SelectedItem changes (source-generated hook)
    partial void OnSelectedItemChanged(Item? value)
    {
        if (value is null) return;
        // Navigate, then clear selection so the same item can be re-selected
        _nav.NavigateAsync("DetailPage", new() { ["Item"] = value });
        SelectedItem = null;
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task RefreshAsync()
    {
        try
        {
            var data = await _api.GetItemsAsync();
            Items = new ObservableCollection<Item>(data);
        }
        finally
        {
            IsRefreshing = false;   // stops the spinner
        }
    }
}
```

**Key bindings explained:**

| Property | Direction | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `ItemsSource` → `Items` | One-way | Supplies the list data |
| `SelectedItem` ↔ `SelectedItem` | Two-way (default) | Captures tap; cleared after navigation so re-selection works |
| `IsRefreshing` ↔ `IsRefreshing` | Two-way | `RefreshView` sets it `true` on pull; you set it `false` when done |
| `Command` → `RefreshCommand` | One-way | Source-generated from `[RelayCommand]` on `RefreshAsync` |

**Important notes:**
- `RefreshView` wraps the `CollectionView` — not the other way around.
- Set `IsRefreshing = false` in a `finally` block so the spinner always stops, even on error.
- Null out `SelectedItem` after navigating; otherwise the same row can't be tapped twice in a row.
